description: linear framebuffer device: set/get independent 32-bit pixels by coordinate
device-tree (FDT) header parsing: magic validation + totalsize/version
pluggable boot flow: platform devices -> bus probe/attach (closure providers) -> registry of device-class endpoints -> service discovery by class; NoDriver/Unavailable typed
write-back block cache: write stays dirty in cache, visible to reads, reaches device only on flush; hit/miss tracked
block-backed FS: multi-block write/read through the device vtable, data persisted on the block device, files on distinct blocks
on-disk FS: format + create + write, then remount and read the file back from the device (persistent superblock/inode/data)
FdSpace (kernel/lib): alloc/reuse, kind/handle, readiness+select, close, BadFd/NoneReady (no sentinels)
pipe FIFO: ordered write/read, full/empty backpressure
in-memory FS: create / write / read-back / lookup / independence / not-found all correct
fd-table VFS over ramfs: open/write/read with positions, re-open, EOF, close, use-after-close
VFS mount switch: mount/umount/resolve dispatches a path to its backing FS (ramfs/diskfs)
MINIX hardening: generation-checked endpoints (stale fails closed), derived runnable state (block reasons), death cleanup (waiter released with DEAD)
memory grant: bounded delegation (OOB rejected) + revocation (stale ref caught)
granttab (kernel/lib): owner-tracked grants, bounded IPC sharing, revoke-on-death
Mailbox<T,N>: post/take, source-filtered take_from, drop-when-full
Registry v2 (kernel/lib): multiple per class, enumerate, gen-checked, unregister-on-death
service (kernel/lib): request/reply over IPC, unknown-tag error reply, stateless loop with handler-held state
argv/envp: packed NUL-terminated argument vector with per-arg offsets
ByteBuf<N>: bounds-checked get + set/copy_from/copy_to return typed OutOfBounds (no silent clamp) on a PAddr
bind() closure captures a pointer + fn, callable with no ctx word/casts; capture mutated across calls
const-generic Ring<T,N> at two capacities (2 and 8): [N]T + % N specialize per instance
dynamic-linking core: R_RISCV_RELATIVE relocations patch a PIE image to its load base
ELF64 parse (header+phdr) + segment load (copy+bss zero-fill) + reject bad magic/truncation
minimal libc core: mc_memeq / mc_strlen / mc_atoi over typed addresses
leveled tracepoints: threshold filtering (dropped count), level+id+value recording, runtime verbosity change
POSIX syscall surface: getpid + open/write/read/close file lifecycle + ENOSYS, over the dispatch table
find_index/any: bounded array search via a closure predicate (found index, not-found N, any)
minimal shell: parses the command word and dispatches builtins (true=0/false=1/unknown=127)
shell: tokenizes a full command line into argv, runs builtins (echo with captured output, true=0/false=1, unknown=127), tolerates extra whitespace
symbol table symbolizes addresses to function+offset (binary search), rejects unsorted/below-first
trace ring buffer: ordered retention, wrap-around (oldest dropped), sequence numbering
postfix-? with else remaps a subsystem error into the layer error type (ok + mapped-err paths)
TTY line discipline (canonical mode): backspace erase + newline-completed line buffering
userland echo utility: joins argv with spaces over the args vector + raw stores
generic Allocator (std/alloc) over a captured bump heap: aligned, advancing allocation
packet Arc-shared between two consumers, freed when the last owner drops (skb/mbuf pattern)
Arc<T> shared ownership: clone adds an owner, last drop frees (handles leak-checked at compile time)
move Arena: bump alloc, reset reclaims+reuses, destroy consumes the linear arena
generational handle resolves live, traps StaleHandle after reset (use-after-reset caught)
Mask32: bounds-checked set/clear/contains, take_first (ascending, removing), out-of-range bit ignored
create<T> typed linear allocation: write/read via address, own_free consumes (leak-checked)
generational pool: use-after-free, double-free, stale-after-reuse all caught (StaleHandle)
generic in-place Ring<T> (instantiated at u32): FIFO push/pop, len, empty/full, wrap-around
SlotMap<T,N>: stable index handles, alloc lowest-free, get/set/free bounds+liveness checked (BadHandle on use-after-free/double-free/oob), Full at capacity
RX scratch from a move Arena (GenRef), per-packet reset, use-after-reset caught
40000 random/UDP-shaped frames parsed with no out-of-bounds read; ok + rejected paths both exercised
driver RX path: parse eth/IPv4/UDP frame -> socket_deliver -> recv payload (and drop unbound-port frames)
UDP sockets: bind (+conflict), deliver demuxed by port (+no-listener), recv payload+sender per socket
TCP state machine: passive + active open handshakes and both close paths transition correctly
TCP reassembly (out-of-order buffering + coalescing, old dropped) + go-back-N retransmit
retransmit timer: arm on send, fire go-back-N at RTO, re-arm, disarm on full ack
TCP segment build/parse (ports/seq/ack/flags/window) + pseudo-header checksum + corruption detection
TCP send window + ACK processing (dup/unsent rejected) + in-order receive + sequence wraparound
UDP datagram build/parse + pseudo-header checksum (incl. corruption detection) correct
info/snapshot service: top-style count/pid/state queries answered over the IPC service loop from a stable proc_snapshot
live update: a service is checkpointed, a new version installed, and state restored into it (data survives the code update)
enforced manifests: a service manifest's IPC allowlist + kcall mask applied to the process, then enforced by ipc_try_send/kcall
POSIX uid/gid + rwx-mode permission checks (owner/group/other, root bypass)
process groups + sessions: setsid/setpgid/getpgid/getsid
scheduler service: kernel ticks/accounts quantum, notifies the sched endpoint on the expiry edge (from=expired task), service refreshes policy
SMP per-core run queues + work stealing: an idle core steals from the busiest (FIFO, load-balanced)
proc_snapshot (kernel/lib): stable pid/state enumeration + state summary, unaffected by later spawn/exit
service supervisor (kernel/lib): declarative manifests (privileges+policy as data), name discovery, restart OnFailure, core failure Fatal
WaitQueue (kernel/lib): block current (park+yield), FIFO wake_one/wake_all, empty no-op, idle yield
page-table-aware user copies: translate VA through Sv39 page table, validate PTE_U/R/W per page; kernel-only page, unmapped hole, and off-page straddle all rejected (nothing copied)
process-death global cleanup: one installed death hook revokes the dead pid's memory grants and unregisters its services on proc_exit (resources cannot outlive their owner)
std/time wrap<u64> timeout arithmetic: wrap-correct elapsed across a u64 rollover and the >= timeout boundary
virtqueue completion fault injection: bad used.id, not-in-flight, and used.len > allocation each rejected with their own distinct typed error
long-running wrap/exhaustion: ring stays FIFO across index wrap, a stale pool handle never revalidates across many generation advances, pool exhaustion fails closed (Full)
std/mmio: RegField get/set/mask + single-bit ops (masked, neighbour-safe) and ordered IO-memory block/word copy with the fence on the correct edge
std/rwlock + std/seqlock: reader-count transitions, uncontended writer acquire, and seqlock read-snapshot retry detection
